title: Reed's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.3)
statement_text: If $G$ is a graph of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ with no clique of size greater than $\omega$, then $\chi(G) \leq \left\lceil \frac{1}{2}(\Delta + 1 + \omega) \right\rceil$.
context_text: Reed's Conjecture, also called Reed's $\omega$, $\Delta$, $\chi$ Conjecture, proposes that the chromatic number is bounded by the average of the trivial greedy upper bound $\Delta+1$ and the clique number $\omega$. Reed proved a partial result (Theorem 1.4) showing $\chi(G) \leq \lceil (1-\varepsilon)(\Delta+1) + \varepsilon\omega \rceil$ for some $\varepsilon > 0$; subsequent work improved $\varepsilon$ to $1/26$ and $1/13$ for large $\Delta$.
paper_contribution: The paper's Theorem 1.6 implies Conjecture 1.3 when $\omega = o\!\left(\frac{\ln\Delta}{\ln\ln\Delta}\right)$, establishing a linear improvement on the greedy bound in this regime and extending the known family of graphs for which the conjecture holds.
paper_title: Bounding $χ$ by a fraction of $Δ$ for graphs without large cliques

title: Question 1.5
statement_text: Does there exist a function $f : \mathbb{R} \to \mathbb{R}$ such that, for every $c > 1$ and every graph $G$ of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ with no clique of size greater than $\Delta^{1/f(c)}$, we have $\chi_\ell(G) \leq \Delta/c$?
context_text: A linear improvement on the greedy bound for graphs without large cliques was notably missing before this paper. Molloy's Theorem 1.2 gives a bound of $200\omega\,\frac{\Delta\ln\ln\Delta}{\ln\Delta}$, and Spencer's Ramsey-theory result shows the bound cannot be improved when $\omega = \Omega(\Delta^{1/2})$, motivating the question of whether a sublinear clique-number condition suffices for a $\Delta/c$ bound on $\chi_\ell$.
notes: The paper answers Question 1.5 in the affirmative via Theorem 1.6, with $f(c) = (72c)^2$ sufficing for large enough $\Delta$.
paper_title: Bounding $χ$ by a fraction of $Δ$ for graphs without large cliques

title: List-chromatic analogue of Reed's Conjecture
statement_text: It is possible that Conjecture 1.3 is also true for the list-chromatic number, i.e., $\chi_\ell(G) \leq \left\lceil \frac{1}{2}(\Delta + 1 + \omega) \right\rceil$ for every graph $G$ of maximum degree at most $\Delta$ with no clique of size greater than $\omega$.
context_text: After stating Reed's Conjecture 1.3 for the ordinary chromatic number and noting Reed's partial result as evidence, the authors remark that the same bound may extend to the list-chromatic number. The correspondence chromatic number is always at least as large as the list-chromatic number, so such an extension would be nontrivial.
notes: Stated informally in the introduction: 'It is possible that Conjecture 1.3 is also true for the list-chromatic number.'
paper_title: Bounding $χ$ by a fraction of $Δ$ for graphs without large cliques
